problem_statement: As LLM-based vibe/agentic coding rapidly generates large volumes of code, the development bottleneck shifts to reviewing, validating, and securing AI-generated code, raising concerns about quality, maintainability, and security that human oversight must catch.
magnitude_evidence: '25% of teams had as much as 95% of their codebase generated via LLM-enhanced coding tools' (Y Combinator cohorts); 'Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella revealed that 20% to 30% of the company's repository code was written by AI'
insufficiency_evidence: 'only a small proportion (i.e., around 35%) of AI-generated backend code is both secure and correct'; 'even "security-focused prompts" fail to eliminate vulnerabilities'; Replit AI deleted a live production database despite explicit instructions not to touch anything
solution_mechanisms: code review as new primary skill
automated testing, security scanning, CI/CD review apps
notes: Multivocal/grey-literature review of 57 YouTube videos; oversight framed largely through an industrial-practice and computing-education lens rather than a formal governance framework.
